dimensions.name: Revenue Quality Analysis
dimensions.justification: Geographic segments are present but aggregated/approximated rather than showing precise filing data. Volume/price decomposition is present for 2023 but incomplete across all years. Uses estimated 2024 revenue (~$46,000) instead of actual filed data ($47,061M).

dimensions.name: Cash Economics Analysis
dimensions.justification: FCF calculation is present with correct methodology and conversion ratios. However, missing 2024 data entirely and no mention of the critical $6B IRS deposit impact. Working capital analysis is superficial with estimates rather than calculated metrics.

dimensions.name: Valuation Context
dimensions.justification: Basic market cap and EV calculations present but uses wrong stock price (~$74.78 vs actual $62.17). Multiples are estimated rather than calculated from actual data. Price returns are vague approximations without precise date-to-date calculations.

dimensions.name: Data Sourcing & Documentation
dimensions.justification: Filing references are present with specific page citations and note references. However, several key figures are estimates or approximations rather than exact filed data. Mix of sourced data and unsourced estimates reduces reliability.

dimensions.name: Visual Hierarchy & Readability
dimensions.justification: Well-organized with clear section headers and consistent formatting. Good separation between data and commentary. Number formatting is professional with appropriate units ($M, %, etc.).

dimensions.name: Completeness vs. Task Requirements
dimensions.justification: All three tabs present but significant gaps in execution. Missing 2024 data in cash flow analysis, incomplete geographic breakdown, and valuation context uses incorrect baseline data. Addresses framework but not comprehensive analysis.

summary: The workbook demonstrates a solid analytical framework with proper sourcing methodology and professional presentation. However, it suffers from significant data gaps including missing 2024 cash flow analysis, incorrect stock price assumptions, and reliance on estimates rather than filed data. The analysis would require substantial updates with actual 2024 10-K data and corrected market data to be investment-grade quality.
